mitigation_templates.name: Fire Safety
mitigation_templates.message_template: If there is an active fire or you see smoke, please call 911 immediately and evacuate the building. Do not use elevators.
safety_instructions: Call 911 immediately
Evacuate via stairwell
Do not use elevators
Close doors behind you
Meet at designated assembly point

mitigation_templates.name: Flood / Water Emergency
mitigation_templates.message_template: If water is actively flooding, try to turn off the water supply valve if safe to do so. Move valuables to higher ground. We are contacting building management now.
safety_instructions: Turn off water supply valve if accessible and safe
Move electronics and valuables away from water
Do not touch electrical outlets near water
Place towels to contain spread if possible

mitigation_templates.name: Gas Leak Safety
mitigation_templates.message_template: If you smell gas, do NOT use any electrical switches, lighters, or open flames. Open windows if safe and evacuate immediately. Call 911 or your gas utility emergency line.
safety_instructions: Do not operate electrical switches or appliances
Do not light matches or lighters
Open windows for ventilation if safe
Evacuate the unit immediately
Call 911 from outside the building

mitigation_templates.name: No Heat Advisory
mitigation_templates.message_template: We understand you have no heat. We are treating this as urgent. In the meantime, use extra blankets and consider using a safe portable heater if available. We are contacting building management.
safety_instructions: Use extra blankets and warm clothing
Close curtains to insulate windows
Never use a stove or oven for heating

mitigation_templates.name: Electrical Safety
mitigation_templates.message_template: Please stay away from any sparking outlets, exposed wires, hot electrical components, or damaged electrical components. Do not touch them. If safe, turn off the breaker for the affected area. We are routing this to emergency maintenance.
safety_instructions: Do not touch exposed wires, sparking outlets, or hot electrical components
Turn off the breaker for the affected area if safe
Keep children and pets away from the area
If a component is hot or smoking, do not attempt to unplug it — turn off the breaker instead
